NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN THE CITY OF BOZEMAN. TREES ALSO FELL ON AND DAMAGED VEHICLES. SOME FENCES BLOWN DOWN. TIME/LOCATION ESTIMATED.
OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTS SOME TREES DOWN IN THE STONE MEADOWS NEIGHBORHOOD ON THE NORTH SIDE OF GREAT FALLS.
VIDEO POSTED ON FACEBOOK SHOWS A TREE GETTING BLOWN DOWN ON SKYLINE DRIVE NE. SOME AREA WEATHER UNDERGROUND STATIONS REPORTED PEAK GUSTS IN THE 50-55MPH RANGE.

***DELAYED REPORT*** LOCAL NEWSPAPER REPORTS FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED ALONG 3RD ST NW BETWEEN THE FAIRGROUNDS AND THE MONTANA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION YARD. WATERS ON THE STREET REACHED 18 INCHES IN DEPTH AND IMPEDED ACCESS TO SEVERAL BUSINESSES IN THE AREA. SEVERAL MOTORISTS WERE STRANDED, AND WATER BEGAN RISING UP THROUGH MANHOLES IN THE STREET.
PART OF AN 8000 BUSHEL GRAIN BIN TWISTED AND RIPPED OFF.
PUBLIC REPORT FROM HAYS, MT THAT THERE WAS PING PONG TO TENNIS BALL SIZE HAIL WHEN THE FIRST SYSTEM MOVED THROUGH.
POST EVENT REPORT. ESTIMATED QUARTER SIZED HAIL. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
TIME IS APPROXIMATED. PART OF TREE TAKEN DOWN.
ALSO, 0.5 INCHES OF RAIN. DOWNPOUR IN ASSOCIATION WITH THUNDERSTORM.

ESTIMATED 50 MPH WIND GUST CAUSING CROP DAMAGE TO MORE TENDER PLANTS. ALSO, SMALL TREE BRANCHES DOWN. BRIEF PEA-SIZED HAIL QUICKLY BECAME RAIN. HEAVY DOWNPOUR PRODUCING 0.25 RAIN MEASURED WITHIN 15 TO 20 MINUTES.
DAMAGE DONE TO TWO VEHICLES IN SANTA RITA. REPORT TIME WAS ESTIMATED VIA RADAR. DELAYED REPORTED RECEIVED ON 7/7.
CORRECTS TIME OF PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 5 SSW SANTA RITA. DELAYED REPORT. SPOTTER REPORTS THAT TWO NEIGHBORS NORTH OF HER REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL WITH THE STORM LAST TUESDAY.

BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED PHOTOS AND VIDEOS OF A LIKELY TORNADO IN THE HAYS AREA. UPDATED INFORMATION FOR THIS REPORT, INCLUDING A MORE PRECISE LAT/LON, TIME, AND EVENT INFORMATION WILL BE PROVIDED WEDNESDAY MORNING...AS NEEDED.
DELAYED REPORT. SPOTTER REPORTS THAT TWO NEIGHBORS NORTH OF HER REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL WITH THE STORM LAST TUESDAY.

POWER POLE DOWN ON TRUCK BYPASS (ROUTE 87). 12 FOOT PIECE OF A ROOF ALSO DAMAGED.

UPDATED. NWS ESTIMATES STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 75 TO 85 MPH THROUGHOUT LEWISTOWN AND NEARBY SURROUNDING AREA. PREVIOUS REPORT: POWER POLE DOWN ON TRUCK BYPASS (ROUTE 87). 12 FOOT PIECE OF A ROOF ALSO DAMAGED.

RECREATION BOAT OVERTURNED AND SANK DURING STRONG THUNDERSTORM. STORM RESULTED IN LARGE WAVES ON THE LAKE WHICH OVERTOOK THE BOAT. 6 ADULT PASSENGERS WERE ON BOARD AND ALL WERE ABLE TO SWIM TO SHORE. IT IS REPORTED THAT THE BOATERS WERE MAKING AN EFFORT TO REACH SHORE BEFORE THE STORM ARRIVED. WIND SPEED ESTIMATE IS BASED OFF NEARBY OBS AND REPORTS.
